heading: Operators and Collectors
body:
MongoDB Search also provides query [MongoDB Search operators](operators-ref) and
[collectors](collectors-ref) that you can use inside the
[$search](pipe.$search) and [$searchMeta](pipe.$searchMeta) aggregation
pipeline stages. The MongoDB Search operators allow you to
locate and retrieve relevant data from the collection on your
cluster. The collector returns a document representing the search
metadata results.

You can use MongoDB Search operators to query terms, phrases, geographic shapes
and points, numeric values, similar documents, synonymous terms, and
more.

You can also search using regex and wildcard expressions. The MongoDB Search
[compound](compound-ref) operator allows you to combine multiple operators
inside your [$search](pipe.$search) stage to perform a complex search and
filter of data based on what *must*, *must not*, or *should* be present
in the documents returned by MongoDB Search. You can use the [compound](compound-ref)
operator to also match or filter documents in the [$search](pipe.$search)
stage itself. Running [$match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/match) after [$search](pipe.$search) is
less performant than running [$search](pipe.$search) with the
[compound](compound-ref) operator.

To learn more about operators and collectors, see [MongoDB Search operators](operators-ref).
